html:
Exclusive to:
Only during the Event: Year of the Catcoon

DeciduousForestSavannaMarshGrassyRockyDesertLunarFestive

Kitcoons are a mob in Don't Starve Together based on Catcoons. The name is a portmanteau of Kitty and Catcoon, latter of which itself is a portmanteau of Cat and Racoon.

Initially, Kitcoons hide behind almost any object spawned in the biomes corresponding to the specific Kitcoon. They can be discovered by their tail wagging behind the object they are hiding under. Some objects, such as twigs, are easier to find a hidden Kitcoon. Additionally, any entity with Kitcoons hiding will have an "investigate" command when hovered over. Interacting with the object (such as picking the grass, mining the boulder, or burning the tree) will also reveal the Kitcoon.

Kitcoons behave similar to Critters by trailing behind a specific player and they are invincible companions. Unlike other critters, there isn't a limit on how many Kitcoons can follow a player.

Initially, the Kitcoons will follow the player who found it; however, Kitcoons will stop following the player if the player dies, leaves the server, or enters the caves. Any neutral Kitcoon will around they were abandoned at and can be bound to another player that pets them. In fact, Kitcoons will start following any player that pets them even if they are already bound to another player. As such, players can adopt any of the 9 Kitcoons at any time by simply petting them. Similarly, a player can choose to unbind the Kitcoons following them anytime by right-clicking on Kitcoons to abandon them. If the player is close to a Kitcoon Nursery, the "abandon" option will be replaced by the "send home" option, and the Kitcoon will stay around the nursery. They won't go far away from the nursery and will interact with each other or the toys around a nursery.

Contents

  • 1 Finding Kitcoons
  • 2 Kitcoon Nursery
    • 2.1 Hide and Seek
    • 2.2 When Event is Over
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Gallery

Finding Kitcoons

Kitcoons are hidden in different Biomes across the Constant, they can be found in the Grasslands, Savanna, Deciduous Forest, Desert, forest, Marsh, Mosaic and on the Lunar Island. They will be hidden behind objects such as trees, saplings, rocks, etc. and their tail will be sticking out from behind whatever they are hiding behind.

The easiest way to locate Kitcoons is to summon and follow a Ticoon. Ticoons can be summoned from an active Catcoon Shrine for 1 Lucky Gold Nugget. In order to get the initial gold nugget to hire the first Ticoon, players may befriend regular catcoon by visiting a Deciduous Forest biome, who pukes a sack of single gold nugget in addition to the regular paraphernalia they puke outside of the event.

There are 9 Kitcoons in total, 8 of which can be found in the biomes listed below. Once all of the Kitcoons are found and sent to the Kitcoon Nursery, the final Kitcoon will appear next to the player.

Kitcoon Locations
Kitcoon Biomes Objects
Deciduous Forest
Forest
Savanna
Swamp
Grasslands
Mosaic
Desert
Lunar Mine
Any

Kitcoon Nursery

The Kitcoon Nursery Kit can be placed to create the Kitcoon Nursery structure. The Kitcoon Nursery is where Kitcoons can be returned after the player has found them. When standing near the Nursery, a "Send Home" option will appear while hovering the mouse over any Kitcoons that currently following the player. Sending a Kitcoon home will cause them to be attached to that Nursery, and they will stay wandering within a certain short range of that Nursery. A player can hover over a Kitcoon which is at home and select the option to "Pet" it, which will cause the Kitcoon to begin following that specific player.

Players can click Kitcoon Nursery to play Hide and Seek mini game with Kitcoons.

Hide and Seek

When there are 3 or more Kitcoons are stayed around a Nursery, the player can click on the Nursery to start the game Hide and Seek. Each Nursery can play one game per day.

To be able to play the game, within 1 to 7.5 tiles radius, there must be hiding places for 5 times the number of Kitcoons in Nursery. Available hiding places includes Structures, Plants, Boulders, Flowers, Caps, etc.

The player has to find all the Kitcoons before time runs out. When time is over, they will be rewarded with Red Pouchs based on how many Kitcoons they found. Finding all 9 Kitcoons will drop Large Red Pouch.

When Event is Over

When not in Year of the Catcoon, the drops will be as following:

Chance Drops
13.5%
4.1%
2.7%
1.4%

Trivia

  • Kitcoons found will follow the player until they are either abandoned or sent home to a Kitcoon Nursery, though they seem similar to Critters in the fact that they avoid any combat and do not take damage. They are slightly smaller than Critters in appearance, and can follow the player in addition to a Critter.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Kitcoons là một mob trong Don't Starve Together dựa trên Catcoons. Cái tên này là từ ghép của Kitty và Catcoon, sau này chính nó là từ ghép của Cat và Racoon.
title: Kitcoon

Like I once was... |wx78 = ITS ATTEMPTING AT IMPROVING ITS HEARING IS PITIFUL |wickerbottom = They possess large ears to facilitate hunting small desert critters. |woodie = Well yer just too cute, aren'tcha? |maxwell = What a pathetic little creature. Those ears are ridiculous. |wigfrid = A wee beastie with nöt-sö-wee ears. |webber = The minister's kit is a golden kit! |winona = A clever little thing. |warly = You're so sweet, I could just eat you up! |wortox = Oh kitty, what big ears you have! |wormwood = Hear me, friend? |wurt = It so little! |walter = Hmm, do you think they'd eat granola? |wanda = Botheration, they're so distractingly cute! |choose = maxwell |source= examining a desert Kitcoon. }} |-|Lunar= {{Quotes |wilson = I must collect more... for research! |willow = You're very small and squeaky. |wolfgang = Is tiny kitty made of... cheese? |wendy = Small and innocent. Like I once was... |wx78 = A FLESHLING. FROM THE MOON? |wickerbottom = A feline? From the moon? It requires dental work. |wigfrid = A wee beastie imbued with a third eye för finding möre hiding spöts. |webber = The minister's kit is a husky kit! |winona = A clever little thing. |warly = You're so sweet, I could just eat you up! |wortox = The kit jumped over the moon, hyuyu! |wormwood = Friend likes Night Ball |wurt = It so little! |walter = Hmm, do you think they'd eat granola? |wanda = Botheration, they're so distractingly cute! |choose = wolfgang |source= examining a lunar Kitcoon. }} |-|Festive= {{Quotes |wilson = I must collect more... for research! |willow = You're very small and squeaky. |wolfgang = Is year of tiny kitty, yes? |wendy = Small and innocent. Like I once was... |wx78 = WHY DO FLESHLINGS CELEBRATE ARBITRARY DATES? |wickerbottom = Oh dear, the bell truly augments your... cuteness. Ho ho! |woodie = Well yer just too cute, aren'tcha? |maxwell = What a pathetic little creature, and in such a gaudy outfit. |wigfrid = A wee beastie with an excellent cöstume. |webber = The minister's kit is an icy kit! |winona = A clever little thing. |warly = You're so sweet, I could just eat you up! |wortox = Oh what a date, let's celebrate! |wormwood = Happy friend |wurt = It so little! |walter = Hmm, do you think they'd eat granola? |wanda = Botheration, they're so distractingly cute! |choose = wanda |source= examining a festive Kitcoon. }} '''Kitcoons''' are a mob in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' based on [[Catcoon]]s. The name is a portmanteau of Kitty and Catcoon, latter of which itself is a portmanteau of Cat and Racoon. Initially, Kitcoons hide behind almost any object spawned in the biomes corresponding to the specific Kitcoon. They can be discovered by their tail wagging behind the object they are hiding under. Some objects, such as twigs, are easier to find a hidden Kitcoon. Additionally, any entity with Kitcoons hiding will have an "investigate" command when hovered over. Interacting with the object (such as picking the grass, mining the boulder, or burning the tree) will also reveal the Kitcoon. Kitcoons behave similar to [[Critters]] by trailing behind a specific player and they are invincible companions. Unlike other critters, there isn't a limit on how many Kitcoons can follow a player. Initially, the Kitcoons will follow the player who found it; however, Kitcoons will stop following the player if the player dies, leaves the server, or enters the caves. Any neutral Kitcoon will around they were abandoned at and can be bound to another player that pets them. In fact, Kitcoons will start following any player that pets them even if they are already bound to another player. As such, players can adopt any of the 9 Kitcoons at any time by simply petting them. Similarly, a player can choose to unbind the Kitcoons following them anytime by right-clicking on Kitcoons to abandon them. If the player is close to a [[#Kitcoon Nursery|Kitcoon Nursery]], the "abandon" option will be replaced by the "send home" option, and the Kitcoon will stay around the nursery. They won't go far away from the nursery and will interact with each other or the toys around a nursery. == Finding Kitcoons == Kitcoons are hidden in different Biomes across the Constant, they can be found in the [[Grasslands]], [[Savanna]], [[Deciduous Forest]], [[Desert]], [[forest]], [[Marsh]], [[Mosaic]] and on the [[Lunar Island]]. They will be hidden behind objects such as trees, saplings, rocks, etc. and their tail will be sticking out from behind whatever they are hiding behind. The easiest way to locate Kitcoons is to summon and follow a [[Ticoon]]. Ticoons can be summoned from an active [[Catcoon Shrine]] for 1 [[Lucky Gold Nugget]]. In order to get the initial gold nugget to hire the first Ticoon, players may befriend regular catcoon by visiting a [[Deciduous Forest]] biome, who pukes a sack of single gold nugget in addition to the regular paraphernalia they puke outside of the event. There are 9 Kitcoons in total, 8 of which can be found in the biomes listed below. Once all of the Kitcoons are found and sent to the Kitcoon Nursery, the final Kitcoon will appear next to the player. {| align="center" border="0" cellpadding="10" cellspacing="20" class="wikitable" style="width: 550px;" |+Kitcoon Locations !Kitcoon !Biomes !Objects |- |style="text-align:center;"|[[File:Kitcoon.png|48px|link=Year of the Catcoon]] |[[Deciduous Forest]] |[[File:Birchnut the Catcoon]] |[[Savanna]] |[[File:Grass Tuft.png|32px|link=Grass Tuft]] |- |style="text-align:center;"|[[File:Swamp Kitcoon.png|48px|link=Year of the Catcoon]] |[[Swamp]] |[[File:Spiky Bush.png|48px|link=Spiky Bush]] |- |style="text-align:center;"|[[File:Moon Kitcoon.png|48px|link=Year of the Catcoon]] |[[Lunar Mine]] |[[File:Boulder.png|48px|link=Boulder]][[File:Gold Vein.png|48px|link=Boulder]][[File:Moonrock boulder.png|48px|link=Boulder]][[File:Moon Glass.png|48px|link=Moon Glass]] |- |style="text-align:center;"|[[File:YOT Kitcoon.png|48px|link=Year of the Catcoon]] |Any |[[File:Kitcoon Nursery.png|48px|link=#Kitcoon Nursery]] |} {{clear}} ==Kitcoon Nursery == {{Object Infobox |canBeBurned = 1 |name = Kitcoon Nursery |image = Kitcoon Nursery.png |perk = Hosts kitcoons. |spawnCode = "kitcoonden" |tool = Can be [[Tools#Hammering_Tools|Hammered]] {{pic|24|Hammer}} }} {{Object Infobox |canBeBurned = 1 |name = Kitcoon Nursery |icon = Kitcoon Nursery Kit.png |image = Kitcoon Nursery Kit Dropped.png|Dropped |description = A home for kitcoons of all stripes. |ingredient1 = Lucky Gold Nugget |multiplier1 = 1 |tier = 28 |perk = Hosts kitcoons. |stack = Does not stack |spawnCode = "kitcoonden_kit" |filter={{Pic|32|Offerings Filter}} }} {{Quotes |wilson = You'd have to be pretty small to fit in there. |willow = They're so small that they all fit. |wolfgang = Is tiny home of tiny kitties. |wendy = Where the wild beasts go. |wx78 = FLESHLING SPAWNER |wickerbottom = A well-constructed habitation for the kittens. |woodie = Looks too much like a bird house for my tastes. |maxwell = A house for kitcoons? Can the pitiful little things not climb trees yet? |wigfrid = A penthöuse för wee beasties. |webber = A nice safe place for kitcoons to rest after playing. |winona = Lofty housing for tiny kits. |warly = Bonjour! Is anyone home? |wortox = Where all the furry mortals go. |wormwood = Where friends live |wurt = It too tiny to fit inside... |walter = Aww, it's like a dog house for cats! |wanda = What a charming little house. |choose = webber}} The '''Kitcoon Nursery Kit''' can be placed to create the '''Kitcoon Nursery''' structure. The Kitcoon Nursery is where Kitcoons can be returned after the player has found them. When standing near the Nursery, a "Send Home" option will appear while hovering the mouse over any Kitcoons that currently following the player. Sending a Kitcoon home will cause them to be attached to that Nursery, and they will stay wandering within a certain short range of that Nursery. A player can hover over a Kitcoon which is at home and select the option to "Pet" it, which will cause the Kitcoon to begin following that specific player. Players can click Kitcoon Nursery to play Hide and Seek mini game with Kitcoons. === Hide and Seek === When there are 3 or more Kitcoons are stayed around a Nursery, the player can click on the Nursery to start the game Hide and Seek. Each Nursery can play one game per day. To be able to play the game, within 1 to 7.5 tiles radius, there must be hiding places for 5 times the number of Kitcoons in Nursery. Available hiding places includes [[Structures]], [[Plants]], [[Boulder]]s, [[Flower]]s, [[Cap]]s, etc. The player has to find all the Kitcoons before time runs out. When time is over, they will be rewarded with [[Red Pouch]]s based on how many Kitcoons they found. Finding all 9 Kitcoons will drop Large Red Pouch. === When Event is Over === When not in Year of the Catcoon, the drops will be as following: {| align="center" border="0" cellpadding="10" cellspacing="20" class="wikitable" style="width: 550px;" !Chance !Drops |- |style="text-align:center;"|13.5% |{{Pic|32|Flint}}{{Pic|32|Cut Grass}}{{Pic|32|Twigs}}{{Pic|32|Petals}} |- |style="text-align:center;"|4.1% |{{Pic|32|Birchnut}}{{Pic|32|Pine Cone}}{{Pic|32|Twiggy Tree Cone}}{{Pic|32|Rocks}}{{Pic|32|Dark Petals}}{{Pic|32|Spoiled Fish Morsel}}{{Pic|32|Crimson Feather}}{{Pic|32|Jet Feather}}{{Pic|32|Saffron Feather}} |- |style="text-align:center;"|2.7% |{{Pic|32|Frayed Yarn}} |- |style="text-align:center;"|1.4% |{{Pic|32|Needlenosed Squirt}}{{Pic|32|Smolt Fry}}{{Pic|32|Gord's Knot}}{{Pic|32|Frazzled Wires}}{{Pic|32|Lucky Cat Jar}} |} =={{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * Kitcoons found will follow the player until they are either abandoned or sent home to a Kitcoon Nursery, though they seem similar to [[Critters]] in the fact that they avoid any combat and do not take damage. They are slightly smaller than Critters in appearance, and can follow the player in addition to a Critter. == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}}Gallery == Kitcoon Nursery Burnt.png|Kitcoon Nursery Burnt Year of the Catcoon Promo.png|Kitcoon promo Year of the Catcoon Ubdate Promo.png|On Update Promo DST Year of the Catcoon Update Promo.gif|On Promo gif Finding Kitcoon.png|Finding a Kitcoon Kitcoons.png|All Types of Kitcoons in Their Biomes All Kitcoons and Some Items.png|All Kitcoons with Some Event Items Kittykit and Kitcoon.png|Kittykit (Left) and Kitcoon (right) RWP TotC Kitcoons Concept Art.png|Kitcoons Concept Art from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Kitcoons Concept Art2.png|Final Kitcoons Concept Art from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Main menu Concept Art.png|Main menu Concept Art from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Main menu Concept Art2.png|Main menu Concept Art from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Kitcoons animation test.gif|Kitcoons animation test from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Hiding Kitcoons animation test.gif|Hiding Kitcoons animation test from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Hiding Kitcoons animation test2.gif|Hiding Kitcoons animation test from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Walking Kitcoons animation test.gif|Walking Kitcoons animation test from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Size Kitcoons animation test.gif|Size Kitcoons from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' RWP TotC Kitcoon Nursery Concept Art.png|Kitcoon Nursery Concept Art from ''Rhymes With Play Dev Cast - Year of the Catcoon'' {{Clear}} Lunar New Year Events= {{Lunar New Year Events}} |-| Mobs= {{Mobs}} [[Category:Lunar New Year]] [[Category:Offerings Filter]]
